headline: FxFiles Goes Fully Browser-Based as FULA Community Expands Integrations
subtitle: Functionland's encrypted vault is now accessible from any browser, positioning the project squarely within a DePIN storage sector generating $150 million in monthly on-chain revenue.
briefing:
Functionland this week completed a meaningful step in consumer accessibility: FxFiles now runs entirely in the web browser, requiring no installation. Users can search, sort, open documents, and play music from a real playlist interface — all from an encrypted decentralized vault reachable from any device. The update builds on last week's launch of browser-based access and compounds Functionland's push to close the gap between decentralized infrastructure and the everyday usability of Google Drive or iCloud.

The timing aligns with an accelerating DePIN storage market. <cite index="1-1">Leading DePIN networks generated roughly $150 million in on-chain revenue in January 2026 alone, paid by real customers for storage deals, compute jobs, and related services.</cite> The broader sector is maturing around a clear consumer thesis: <cite index="3-22,3-23">the most successful DePIN projects are becoming "boring in the best way possible" — users don't think about the underlying blockchain, they just use a cheaper, more private service.</cite> Functionland's browser-first FxFiles move fits that mold precisely.

On the community side, the FULA network's open-source contributors continued completing new integrations this week, underscoring that <cite index="19-2,19-3">Functionland's GitHub organization lists over 115 repositories, reflecting ongoing engineering work across the protocol layer, the FxFiles client, and node software.</cite> The network currently counts 26 active nodes.

As the DePIN storage vertical shifts toward <cite index="2-3">"useful, paid storage" with verifiable, high-quality service and on-chain paid deals as the ecosystem's focus</cite>, watch for whether Functionland's no-friction browser experience translates into measurable node and user growth over the coming weeks.
